title: Fault scenario (Contract 6)
description: Shared fault-scenario vocabulary for milestone I7. Each scenario encodes: stable ID, injection description, expected gateway semantics, expected client-visible behavior, metrics that must move, and an abort condition. Consumers: inferops (injects), infergate (semantics tests), inferbench (client-impact measurement), inference-lab (postmortems). The 12 required scenarios ship as fixtures under examples/faults/ (fs-01 … fs-12); their semantics are normative — an injection campaign that observes different behavior has found a defect (in the implementation or, via the contract-defect process, in the scenario).

scenario_id.description: Stable scenario ID (fs-01 … fs-12 for the Contract 6 catalog). IDs are contract surface: postmortems, dashboards, and campaign reports key on them.

contract_item.description: Which numbered item of the Contract 6 catalog this scenario encodes (coverage audit: each of 1-12 appears exactly once across the fixtures).

injection.description: What inferops does to induce the fault. Descriptive, tool-agnostic: the injection method may vary; the induced condition may not.

method.description: Suggested mechanism (e.g. process-kill, latency-injection, load-profile); informative.

parameters.description: Injection parameters (free-form, scenario-specific; e.g. signal, phase, delay_ms, fraction).

expected_gateway_semantics.description: Normative statements about what the gateway MUST do under this fault (infergate's semantics tests assert these).

expected_client_visible_behavior.description: Normative statements about what clients observe (inferbench measures these).

metrics_that_must_move.description: Signals that MUST visibly respond to the injection. An injection whose expected metrics do not move is either not injected or not observable — both fail the campaign.

metrics_that_must_not_move.description: Signals that MUST stay flat (e.g. retries after first token). Violations are defects even when everything else looks healthy.

abort_condition.description: Condition under which the injection MUST be aborted and the system restored (safety rail for the campaign). An abort is recorded, never silently retried.

metricExpectation.description: One observable signal and its expected movement. source=gateway/client: metric MUST be a name from the Contract 2 vocabulary (metrics/metrics.md; client-side series carry the client_ prefix). source=engine: metric MUST be a canonical signal key resolved via the backend-capability name_mapping (never an engine-native metric name — those vary by engine version). source=platform: an orchestration-level metric (e.g. kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total).

expectation.description: Expected movement, including relevant label constraints (e.g. increases with stage=pre-first-token).
